title: Expelled from Paradise: Resonance Anime Movie Reveals Key Visual, Teaser Trailer
content:
The previously announced Expelled from Paradise: Resonance anime movie revealed a key visual and teaser trailer at AnimeJapan 2026. Toei Animation is animating the series.

See more AnimeJapan 2026 updates: AnimeJapan 2026 All Announcements and Highlights

Seiji Mizushima is directing the film and Gen Urobuchi (NITROPLUS) is in charge of the screenplay. Masatsugu Saito is providing the character designs, with the mechanical designs done by Kanetake Ebikawa, Junya Ishigaki and Takayuki Yanase. NARASAKI is composing the music. The main cast includes:

• Ayane Sakura as Gabriel

• Saori Hayami as Arma

• Yoshino Aoyama as Daniel

• Sally Amaki as Raguel

Aniplex of USA has licensed the original Expelled from Paradise movie for its North American theatrical release, as well as for its Blu-Ray/DVD release. They describe the plot as follows: With Earth now left in ruins following the “Nano Hazard,” most of humanity has abandoned the planet they once called home along with their physical bodies and rebuilt their digitalized minds into a society within the cyber universe of “DEVA.” A.D. 2400, DEVA’s central council detects an incident of unauthorized access into their mainframe. Someone on Earth was trying to hack into the system. The only information DEVA was able to retrieve was that the hacker referred to themselves as “Frontier Setter.” To investigate the mysterious hacker’s motives, the high officials of DEVA dispatch System Security Third Officer Angela Balzac to the Earth’s surface. Equipped with a prosthetic “material body,” Angela attempts to make contact with a local agent Dingo, but what awaited her instead was a swarm of Sandworms now infesting the Earth’s surface. Angela intercepts the gruesome pests with her exoskeletal powered suit Arhan. Will Angela and Dingo be able to find Frontier Setter on this devastated planet? Their journey to explore the secrets of the world will begin now…!
author: Anime Corner

title: Let's Go Kaikigumi Anime Releases New Trailer
content:
Let’s Go Kaikigumi released a new trailer along with a July 5 premiere date. The trailer was first shown at this year’s AnimeJapan 2026 event. Additionally, Mayumi Shintani joins the cast as Cursed Doll.

Check out more AnimeJapan 2026 updates: AnimeJapan 2026 All Announcements and Highlights

C-Station is animating the series and Yutaka Hirata is the director. Mutsumi Ito writing the scripts and Yoshimitsu Yamashita is designing the characters. The cast includes:

• Tasuku Hatanaka as the unnamed protagonist

• Ruriko Aoki as Mechako

• Yoshino Aoyama as Shurako

• Yuki Sakakihara as Doppelganger

• Shigeru Chiba as narrator

The anime is adapting the manga series written and illustrated by Byu. It was serialized on Omocoro from July 2018 to July 2024. Shogakukan released five volumes between September 2021 and August 2024. The horror comedy series follows Mechako, who is the leader of the supernatural organization called Kaikigumi. When an easily scared, unnamed protagonist encounters her, he became part of the organization. Source: Let’s go Kaikigumi anime official website , AnimeJapan 2026 © Byu, Burg Hamburg Burg / Shogakukan Creative / Kanto Kaiki-gumi
author: Anime Corner

title: Made in Abyss: Awakening Mystery Anime Reveals New Key Visual, October 23 Premiere
content:
Made in Abyss anime revealed a new key visual for Part 1 of its theatrical film series, Awakening Mystery . The first installment is set to premiere in Japan on October 23, 2026.

It was also announced that ahead of the new film series, previous Made in Abyss recap movies and entries will receive theatrical re-screenings in Japan. This includes the compilation films Journey’s Dawn and Wandering Twilight , along with Dawn of the Deep Soul and the TV anime sequel The Golden City of the Scorching Sun .

You can see the new visual below.

The anime movie previously revealed a teaser visual , featuring two new characters, Tepaste and Cravagli, shown at the Tumble Tavern. The new film will serve as a direct continuation of the 2022 TV anime Made in Abyss: The Golden City of the Scorching Sun . Kinema Citrus returns as the animation studio, continuing its involvement from the previous entries in the series. The staff includes:

• Director: Masayuki Kojima

• Screenplay: Hideyuki Kurata

• Character Design: Yuka Kuroda, Kazuchika Kise (Production I.G)

• Animation Production: Kinema Citrus

The confirmed voice cast includes:

• Miyu Tomita as Riko

• Mariya Ise as Reg

• Shiori Izawa as Nanachi

• Misaki Kuno as Faputa

You can watch the first trailer below.

Made in Abyss , written and illustrated by Akihito Tsukushi, follows the descent of a girl named Riko and a robot boy named Reg into the Abyss, a massive pit filled with ancient relics and mysterious creatures. The manga is serialized on Takeshobo’s Web Comic Gamma and has over 22 million circulating copies.

A film concert titled Made in Abyss Film Concert: Deep Echoes of Resonance was held today on February 8, 2026 at Tokyo’s Nakano ZERO Grand Hall. Composer Kevin Penkin conducted a live orchestra performing the anime’s soundtrack, accompanied by anime footage on a large screen. Vocalist Ko Saito and KIHOW (MYTH & ROID), who performed the series’ theme songs, also appeared for live performances. Main cast members Miyu Tomita (Riko), Mariya Ise (Reg), and Shiori Izawa (Nanachi) were also present.
author: Anime Corner

title: Dr. Stone Final Season Reveals Episode 25 Preview Trailer and Images
content:
Dr. Stone: Science Future (Season 4) Cour 3 (final) revealed the preview images and trailer for Episode 1 (Episode 25 of the season). The episode is titled “Future Engine,” and it’s scheduled for an April 2 premiere date .

You can watch the preview trailer below.

You can see some more images below.

The anime previously revealed the main trailer and visual , along with the opening theme song by ASIAN KUNG-FU GENERATION. Ryusui’s older brother Sai was also introduced with a “powered up” teaser trailer , while Seichiro Yamashita was later revealed as his voice actor. You can watch the main trailer below.

The story follows a genius scientist, Senku Ishigami, as the he attempts to revive humanity after everyone gets mysteriously petrified. Having slowly achieved remarkable progress, their goal is now space, but Dr. Xeno proved to be a new challenge along with Stanley Snyder. They eventually joined the Kingdom Science, and now it’s time to learn the secret behind Why-Man.

The first season of the Dr. Stone anime adaptation began airing in the Summer of 2019 anime season and had 24 episodes. The second season, Dr. Stone: Stone Wars , started in January 2021 and ran for 11 episodes. The series also received a special episode focusing on Ryusui in July 2022. The third, Stone: New World , ran for 23 episodes over 2 split cours in 2023. The fourth season was announced as the final one immediately after the finale of Season 3.
author: Anime Corner

title: Farming Life in Another World 2 Anime Reveals 2nd Trailer, 6 More Cast
content: Pony Canyon's stage presentation at the AnimeJapan 2026 event on Sunday revealed the key visual, more cast members, and second trailer for Farming Life in Another World 2, the second anime season based on Kinosuke Naito's Farming Life in Another World (Isekai Nonbiri Nōka) light novel series. Image courtesy of Pony Canyon©Kinosuke Naito/Farming Life in Another World2 Production CommitteeThe newly announced cast members are: Hikaru Midorikawa as The Demon God Image via Farming Life in Another World anime's website©Kinosuke Naito/Farming Life in Another World2 Production CommitteeRikuya Yasuda as Jack (left in image below)Eri Yukimura as Morte Image via Farming Life in Another World anime's website©Kinosuke Naito/Farming Life in Another World2 Production CommitteeMariko Honda as Bell Forgma Image via Farming Life in Another World anime's website©Kinosuke Naito/Farming Life in Another World2 Production CommitteeYasuhiro Mamiya as Gou Forgma Image via Farming Life in Another World anime's website©Kinosuke Naito/Farming Life in Another World2 Production CommitteeFumiya Imai as Kuzden Image via Farming Life in Another World anime's website©Kinosuke Naito/Farming Life in Another World2 Production CommitteePony Canyon also announced the anime's North American premiere screening at the Anime Boston 2026 event on April 4. Image courtesy of Pony Canyon©Kinosuke Naito/Farming Life in Another World2 Production CommitteeThe new season will premiere on the TV Tokyo channel on April 6 at 26:00 (effectively, April 7 at 2:00 a.m.), and it will also run on BS TV Tokyo, AT-X, Tulip-TV, TV-U Yamagata, and Hokuriku Broadcasting, as well as on the Amazon Prime Video streaming service exclusively in Japan. HIDIVE will begin streaming the season on April 7.The two seasons star:Atsushi Abe as Hiraku MachioShino Shimoji as LuuAya Suzaki as TierLynn as RiaMiyu Tomita as FloraYukiyo Fujii as AnnMachico as SennaNatsumi Hioka as RasutisumoonThe new season's added cast members include:Natsu Yorita as RashashiMisaki Watada as IguHiyori Kōno as MamuMariko Higashiuchi as GruwaldTōru Sakurai as GordonGenta Nakamura as NarfHiroshi Shirokuma as DossHaruka Shiraishi as HitertoYō Taichi as FooshRumi Ōkubo as KupidJun Fukuyama as DomimeSaori Ōnishi as KuonAsuna Tomari as the GiantsMisaki Kuno as Spirit KingRyōichi Kuraya (Tsugumomo) is directing the new season at Zero-G, and also replacing Tōko Machida on supervising and writing the series scripts. Yoshiko Saitо̄ (Assassins Pride) is returning as the chief animation director and character designer. Yasuharu Takanashi (Fairy Tail) and Johannes Nilsson (Tsukimichi -Moonlit Fantasy-) is back to compose the music, with Pony Canyon producing.You Natsuki serves as the sub-character designer, and Atsushi Shibata and jimao are designing the props. Yū Okimoto replaces Nozomu Ōma as the compositing director of photography. Tomoki Murakami is handling the 2D and visual effects.Fumiyuki Gō replaces Shūhei Abe as sound director, and Bit Groove Promotion replaces Saber Links on sound production.Lulucy Luu (as voiced by Shino Shimoji) and Tier (Aya Suzaki) once again perform an opening theme song, “It's a beautiful story,” and Yui Hizuki is also back with a new ending theme song, “Sunny Steps.”One Peace Books began releasing Yasuyuki Tsurugi's manga adaptation of the novels in November 2020, and it describes the story:After Hiraku dies of a serious illness, God brings him back to life, gives his health and youth back, and sends him to a fantasy world of his choice. In order to enjoy his second shot, God bestows upon him the almighty farming tool! Watch as Hiraku digs, chops, and ploughs in another world in this laidback farming fantasy!The first season premiered on the AT-X channel on January 6, 2023 at 9:00 p.m. (7:00 a.m. EST), and it also ran on TV Tokyo and BS TV Tokyo. HIDIVE streamed the anime as it airs in Japan.Naito began serializing the story on the Shōsetsuka ni Narō website in 2016, where it is ongoing. Kadokawa began publishing the novels in print with illustrations by Yasumo (Banished From The Heroes' Party) in October 2017, and published the 20th novel on February 28. Tsurugi launched the manga in 2017, and Kadokawa published the 16th volume on March 9.A four-panel comedy spinoff manga titled Isekai Nonbiri Nōka no Nichijō (Farming Life in Another World: Daily Life) by Yuji launched in Kadokawa's Dragon Age magazine on July 8, 2022, and the seventh volume shipped on March 9.Sources: AnimeJapan 2026 stage presentation, Press release
author: ANN

contentBlocks.text: Pony Canyon 's stage presentation at the AnimeJapan 2026 event on Sunday revealed the key visual, more cast members, and second trailer for Farming Life in Another World 2 , the second anime season based on Kinosuke Naito 's Farming Life in Another World ( Isekai Nonbiri Nōka ) light novel series.

contentBlocks.credit: Image courtesy of Pony Canyon

contentBlocks.text: The newly announced cast members are:
Hikaru Midorikawa as The Demon God

contentBlocks.credit: Image via Farming Life in Another World anime's website

contentBlocks.text: Rikuya Yasuda as Jack (left in image below) Eri Yukimura as Morte

contentBlocks.credit: Image via Farming Life in Another World anime's website
Image via Farming Life in Another World anime's website
Image via Farming Life in Another World anime's website
Image via Farming Life in Another World anime's website

contentBlocks.text: Pony Canyon also announced the anime's North American premiere screening at the Anime Boston 2026 event on April 4.

contentBlocks.credit: Image courtesy of Pony Canyon

contentBlocks.text: The new season's added cast members include:

Ryōichi Kuraya ( Tsugumomo ) is directing the new season at Zero-G , and also replacing Tōko Machida on supervising and writing the series scripts. Yoshiko Saitо̄ ( Assassins Pride ) is returning as the chief animation director and character designer. Yasuharu Takanashi ( Fairy Tail ) and Johannes Nilsson ( Tsukimichi -Moonlit Fantasy- ) is back to compose the music, with Pony Canyon producing.

You Natsuki serves as the sub-character designer, and Atsushi Shibata and jimao are designing the props. Yū Okimoto replaces Nozomu Ōma as the compositing director of photography. Tomoki Murakami is handling the 2D and visual effects.
Fumiyuki Gō replaces Shūhei Abe as sound director, and Bit Groove Promotion replaces Saber Links on sound production.

Lulucy Luu (as voiced by Shino Shimoji ) and Tier ( Aya Suzaki ) once again perform an opening theme song, “It's a beautiful story,” and Yui Hizuki is also back with a new ending theme song, “Sunny Steps.”
One Peace Books began releasing Yasuyuki Tsurugi 's manga adaptation of the novels in November 2020, and it describes the story:

After Hiraku dies of a serious illness, God brings him back to life, gives his health and youth back, and sends him to a fantasy world of his choice. In order to enjoy his second shot, God bestows upon him the almighty farming tool! Watch as Hiraku digs, chops, and ploughs in another world in this laidback farming fantasy!
The first season premiered on the AT-X channel on January 6, 2023 at 9:00 p.m. (7:00 a.m. EST), and it also ran on TV Tokyo and BS TV Tokyo . HIDIVE streamed the anime as it airs in Japan.

Naito began serializing the story on the Shōsetsuka ni Narō website in 2016, where it is ongoing. Kadokawa began publishing the novels in print with illustrations by Yasumo ( Banished From The Heroes' Party ) in October 2017, and published the 20th novel on February 28. Tsurugi launched the manga in 2017, and Kadokawa published the 16th volume on March 9.

A four-panel comedy spinoff manga titled Isekai Nonbiri Nōka no Nichijō ( Farming Life in Another World : Daily Life) by Yuji launched in Kadokawa 's Dragon Age magazine on July 8, 2022, and the seventh volume shipped on March 9.

title: Suikoden Anime Announces 8 More Cast Members
content: The special stage event for Suikoden: The Anime, the adaptation of Konami's Suikoden II game's story, announced more cast members and unveiled the anime's fourth image visual at AnimeJapan 2026 on Sunday. Image via Suikoden: The Anime's website©Konami Digital Entertainment / Suikoden : The Anime ProjectThe newly announced cast members are: Jun Kasama as Culgan Yoshitsugu Matsuoka as Seed Megumi Han as Leknaat Shōta Hayama as Gengen Ryūnosuke Watanuki as Rikimaru Saya Hitomi as Rina Mizutama Umino as Eilie Yūsuke Sasaki as Bolgan Previously announced cast members include:Toshiki Kumagai as Riliu Image via Suikoden: The Anime's website©Konami Digital Entertainment / Suikoden : The Anime Project Shimba Tsuchiya as Jowy Image via Suikoden: The Anime's website©Konami Digital Entertainment / Suikoden : The Anime Project Ayumi Hinohara as Nanami Image via Suikoden: The Anime's website©Konami Digital Entertainment / Suikoden : The Anime Project Katsuyuki Konishi as Viktor Image via Suikoden: The Anime's website©Konami Digital Entertainment / Suikoden : The Anime Project Yūichi Nakamura as Flik Image via Suikoden: The Anime's website©Konami Digital Entertainment / Suikoden : The Anime Project Taku Yashiro as Luca BlightImage via Suikoden: The Anime's website©Konami Digital Entertainment / Suikoden : The Anime Project Chika Anzai as Jillia BlightImage via Suikoden: The Anime's website©Konami Digital Entertainment / Suikoden : The Anime Project Akihiro Mine as RowdImage via Suikoden: The Anime's website©Konami Digital Entertainment / Suikoden : The Anime Project Tsukino Chikasada as PilikaImage via Suikoden: The Anime's website©Konami Digital Entertainment / Suikoden : The Anime Project Ayumu Murase as LucImage via Suikoden: The Anime's website©Konami Digital Entertainment / Suikoden : The Anime Project Takeo Ōtsuka as ShuImage via Suikoden: The Anime's website©Konami Digital Entertainment / Suikoden : The Anime Project Yoshino Aoyama as AppleImage via Suikoden: The Anime's website©Konami Digital Entertainment / Suikoden : The Anime Project Masato Niwa as MukumukuImage via Suikoden: The Anime's website©Konami Digital Entertainment / Suikoden : The Anime ProjectYūzō Satō (credited as just YUZO on the anime's English website) is directing the anime at KONAMI animation, the game studio's anime studio. NBCUniversal Entertainment Japan anime and music production department member Ryō Hino serves as the producer and production manager.Michihiro Tsuchiya (PriPara, ClassicaLoid) is overseeing the series scripts. Fumi Ishikawa from Konami Digital Entertainment is credited as the game character designer, Arata Suzuki from Konami Digital Entertainment is credited for the original drafts of the anime character designs, and Ryō Yamauchi (My Master Has No Tail; Please Put Them On, Takamine-san!) is the anime character designer. Yoshikazu Iwanami (Psycho-Pass, Ghost in the Shell Arise) is the sound director, and Kōji Nakamura (EUREKA SEVEN AO, Ping Pong live-action film) is composing the music.Konami describes Suikoden II:Our story's hero and his friend Jowy are members of the Unicorn Youth Brigade, which participated in a drawn-out border dispute between the Highland Kingdom and the City-States of Jowstown. A few months earlier, a truth agreement was signed between the two forces, both of which were happy to see an end to the fighting. However, hidden under the momentary peace the fires of a new war continued to smolder…Suikoden I&II HD Remaster Gate Rune and Dunan Unification Wars, the HD remastered release of Konami's classic role-playing games Suikoden and Suikoden II, launched for Nintendo Switch, PlayStation 5, PlayStation 4, Xbox Series X|S, Xbox One, and PC via Steam on March 6.Konami released the original game for the PlayStation in 1995, followed by the sequel game in 1998. The series has five main installments and five spinoff games.Konami is also developing the new mobile and PC game Gensō Suikoden: Star Leap, and a stage play adaptation of Suikoden I that will run in December in Tokyo and Kyoto. Sources: Suikoden anime's website, Comic Natalie
author: ANN

contentBlocks.text: The special stage event for Suikoden: The Anime , the adaptation of Konami 's Suikoden II game's story, announced more cast members and unveiled the anime's fourth image visual at AnimeJapan 2026 on Sunday.

contentBlocks.text: Yūzō Satō (credited as just YUZO on the anime's English website) is directing the anime at KONAMI animation , the game studio's anime studio. NBCUniversal Entertainment Japan anime and music production department member Ryō Hino serves as the producer and production manager.

Michihiro Tsuchiya ( PriPara , ClassicaLoid ) is overseeing the series scripts. Fumi Ishikawa from Konami Digital Entertainment is credited as the game character designer, Arata Suzuki from Konami Digital Entertainment is credited for the original drafts of the anime character designs, and Ryō Yamauchi ( My Master Has No Tail ; Please Put Them On, Takamine-san! ) is the anime character designer. Yoshikazu Iwanami ( Psycho-Pass , Ghost in the Shell Arise ) is the sound director, and Kōji Nakamura ( EUREKA SEVEN AO , Ping Pong live-action film) is composing the music.

Our story's hero and his friend Jowy are members of the Unicorn Youth Brigade, which participated in a drawn-out border dispute between the Highland Kingdom and the City-States of Jowstown. A few months earlier, a truth agreement was signed between the two forces, both of which were happy to see an end to the fighting. However, hidden under the momentary peace the fires of a new war continued to smolder…

Suikoden I&II HD Remaster Gate Rune and Dunan Unification Wars , the HD remastered release of Konami 's classic role-playing games Suikoden and Suikoden II , launched for Nintendo Switch, PlayStation 5, PlayStation 4, Xbox Series X|S, Xbox One, and PC via Steam on March 6.
Konami released the original game for the PlayStation in 1995, followed by the sequel game in 1998. The series has five main installments and five spinoff games.
Konami is also developing the new mobile and PC game Gensō Suikoden : Star Leap , and a stage play adaptation of Suikoden I that will run in December in Tokyo and Kyoto.

title: 'Nijusseiki Denki Mokuroku' Reveals Additional Cast, Staff, Theme Songs, Second Promo
content:
The Nijusseiki Denki Mokuroku ( Sparks of Tomorrow ) Blue Stage revealed additional cast, staff, theme songs, second key visual (pictured), and a second promotional video on Sunday. The anime, adapting Hiro Yuuki 's historical romance light novel , is scheduled to premiere on July 5 at 11:00 p.m. on Tokyo MX, followed by BS11, ABC TV, and TV Aichi.

Cast Suzu Harashima: You Taichi ( Majo to Yajuu ) Kate Ookura: Natsumi Kawaida ( Hige wo Soru. Soshite Joshikousei wo Hirou. ) Yajirou Yagura: Kazuki Ura ( Gachiakuta ) Izou Masubushi: Daisuke Hirakawa ( Kimetsu no Yaiba )

Staff Art Director: Mao Takayama ( Kobayashi-san Chi no Maid Dragon S background art) Color Design: Fuyuki Ootsuka Prop Design: Aya Hikita ( Hibike! Euphonium 3 key animation) Director of Photography: Kouki Ueda ( City The Animation ) 3D Director: Mitsuki Takaki Sound Director: Youta Tsuruoka ( Magia Record: Mahou Shoujo Madoka☆Magica Gaiden ) Music Production: Lantis , Heart Company

Singer-songwriter Luna Goami ( Shuumatsu Touring ending theme) is performing the opening theme "Eureka Evrika," which is previewed in the promotional video below. American singer-songwriter Ginger Root performs the ending theme.

Minoru Oota ( Kobayashi-san Chi no Maid Dragon S episode director) is helming the anime at Kyoto Animation . Tatsuhiko Urahata ( Ookami to Koushinryou: Merchant Meets the Wise Wolf ) is handling the series composition. Kouhei Okamura ( Free! Movie: The Final Stroke ) is designing the characters and serving as chief animation director, while Takaaki Suzuki ( Violet Evergarden ) is in charge of world design. Hitomi Koto is composing the music.

The light novel won Honorable Mention at the 8th Kyoto Animation Award in 2017. Yuuki published the title under Kyoto Animation KA Esuma Bunko label with illustrations by Kazumi Ikeda ( Hibike! Euphonium 3 character design) in August 2018.

PV 2
author: MyAnimeList

Cast Suzu Harashima: You Taichi ( Majo to Yajuu ) Kate Ookura: Natsumi Kawaida ( Hige wo Soru. Soshite Joshikousei wo Hirou. ) Yajirou Yagura: Kazuki Ura ( Gachiakuta ) Izou Masubushi: Daisuke Hirakawa ( Kimetsu no Yaiba )

Staff Art Director: Mao Takayama ( Kobayashi-san Chi no Maid Dragon S background art) Color Design: Fuyuki Ootsuka Prop Design: Aya Hikita ( Hibike! Euphonium 3 key animation) Director of Photography: Kouki Ueda ( City The Animation ) 3D Director: Mitsuki Takaki Sound Director: Youta Tsuruoka ( Magia Record: Mahou Shoujo Madoka☆Magica Gaiden ) Music Production: Lantis , Heart Company

Singer-songwriter Luna Goami ( Shuumatsu Touring ending theme) is performing the opening theme "Eureka Evrika," which is previewed in the promotional video below. American singer-songwriter Ginger Root performs the ending theme.

Minoru Oota ( Kobayashi-san Chi no Maid Dragon S episode director) is helming the anime at Kyoto Animation . Tatsuhiko Urahata ( Ookami to Koushinryou: Merchant Meets the Wise Wolf ) is handling the series composition. Kouhei Okamura ( Free! Movie: The Final Stroke ) is designing the characters and serving as chief animation director, while Takaaki Suzuki ( Violet Evergarden ) is in charge of world design. Hitomi Koto is composing the music.

The light novel won Honorable Mention at the 8th Kyoto Animation Award in 2017. Yuuki published the title under Kyoto Animation KA Esuma Bunko label with illustrations by Kazumi Ikeda ( Hibike! Euphonium 3 character design) in August 2018.

title: Rascal Does Not Dream of a Dear Friend Film's Teaser Reveals October 16 Release Date
content: Aniplex unveiled a new teaser trailer for Seishun Buta Yarō wa Dear Friend no Yume o Minai (Rascal Does Not Dream of a Dear Friend) film, the latest anime adaptation of Hajime Kamoshida's Rascal Does Not Dream (Seishun Buta Yarō or Aobuta) novel series, during its stage event for the film at AnimeJapan 2026 on Sunday. The teaser reveals the film's October 16 date. Image courtesy of Aniplex©2025 鴨志田 一/KADOKAWA/青ブタ ProjectAniplex also revealed a new version of the film's previously unveiled visual (shown right) which contains the release date.The film's returning cast includes:Kaito Ishikawa as Sakuta AzusagawaAsami Seto as Mai SakurajimaSora Amamiya as Uzuki HirokawaAya Yamane as Ikumi AkagiKonomi Kohara as Sara HimejiManaka Iwami as Miori MitōReina Ueda as Nene IwamizawaNao Tōyama as Tomoe KogaAtsumi Tanezaki as Rio FutabaMaaya Uchida as Nodoka ToyohamaYurika Kubo as Kaede AzusagawaInori Minase as Shōko MakinoharaSōichi Masui returns to direct the film at CloverWorks. Masahiro Yokotani is also back to oversee and write the script, and Satomi Tamura is again designing the characters. The band fox capture plan also returns to compose the music. Aniplex is once again distributing the film. The new film is teased as taking the story towards its finale. (The film shares the same name as the 15th and final novel volume.)Rascal Does Not Dream of Bunny Girl Senpai (Seishun Buta Yarō wa Bunny Girl-senpai no Yume wo Minai), the first television anime based on the light novel series, premiered in Japan in October 2018 and aired for 13 episodes. The anime adapts the story up to the fifth novel volume. Aniplex of America streamed the series on Crunchyroll, Funimation, and other services.Rascal Does Not Dream of a Dreaming Girl (Seishun Buta Yarō wa Yume-Miru Shōjo no Yume wo Minai), the anime film that adapts the the sixth and seventh light novel volumes in the franchise, opened in Japan in June 2019. Aniplex of America and Funimation Films screened the anime in the United States. Crunchyroll began streaming the film on June 19 with both English subtitles and dub.The first film Rascal Does Not Dream of a Sister Venturing Out opened in Japan in June 2023. The film sold 118,108 tickets in its first three days and earned 175,648,924 yen (about US$1.28 million) to rank #4 in its opening weekend. Rascal Does Not Dream of a Knapsack Kid, the second film, opened in Japan in December 2023, and ranked at #4 in its opening weekend. The film sold 119,000 tickets and earned 164,594,554 yen (about US$1.11 million) in its first three days.Rascal Does Not Dream of Santa Claus (Seishun Buta Yarō wa Santa Claus no Yume wo Minai), the television anime adapting the light novel series' "University Student Arc," premiered on July 5. Crunchyroll streamed the anime as it aired, and is also streaming an English dub.The original novel series has over 3 million copies in circulation. The Yen On imprint of Yen Press publishes the novel series, and Yen Press publishes Tsugumi Nanamiya's manga adaptation. Yen On will publish the Rascal Does Not Dream of a Dear Friend novel volume on January 6.Sources: AnimeJapan 2026 panel, press release, Disclosure: Kadokawa World Entertainment (KWE), a wholly owned subsidiary of Kadokawa Corporation, is the majority owner of Anime News Network, LLC. One or more of the companies mentioned in this article are part of the Kadokawa Group of Companies.
author: ANN

contentBlocks.text: Aniplex unveiled a new teaser trailer for Seishun Buta Yarō wa Dear Friend no Yume o Minai ( Rascal Does Not Dream of a Dear Friend ) film, the latest anime adaptation of Hajime Kamoshida 's Rascal Does Not Dream ( Seishun Buta Yarō or Aobuta) novel series, during its stage event for the film at AnimeJapan 2026 on Sunday. The teaser reveals the film's October 16 date.

contentBlocks.credit: Image courtesy of Aniplex

contentBlocks.text: Aniplex also revealed a new version of the film's previously unveiled visual (shown right) which contains the release date.

Sōichi Masui returns to direct the film at CloverWorks . Masahiro Yokotani is also back to oversee and write the script, and Satomi Tamura is again designing the characters. The band fox capture plan also returns to compose the music. Aniplex is once again distributing the film.
The new film is teased as taking the story towards its finale. (The film shares the same name as the 15th and final novel volume.)

Rascal Does Not Dream of Bunny Girl Senpai ( Seishun Buta Yarō wa Bunny Girl-senpai no Yume wo Minai ), the first television anime based on the light novel series, premiered in Japan in October 2018 and aired for 13 episodes. The anime adapts the story up to the fifth novel volume. Aniplex of America streamed the series on Crunchyroll , Funimation , and other services.

Rascal Does Not Dream of a Dreaming Girl ( Seishun Buta Yarō wa Yume-Miru Shōjo no Yume wo Minai ), the anime film that adapts the the sixth and seventh light novel volumes in the franchise , opened in Japan in June 2019. Aniplex of America and Funimation Films screened the anime in the United States. Crunchyroll began streaming the film on June 19 with both English subtitles and dub .

The first film Rascal Does Not Dream of a Sister Venturing Out opened in Japan in June 2023. The film sold 118,108 tickets in its first three days and earned 175,648,924 yen (about US$1.28 million) to rank #4 in its opening weekend. Rascal Does Not Dream of a Knapsack Kid , the second film, opened in Japan in December 2023, and ranked at #4 in its opening weekend. The film sold 119,000 tickets and earned 164,594,554 yen (about US$1.11 million) in its first three days.

Rascal Does Not Dream of Santa Claus ( Seishun Buta Yarō wa Santa Claus no Yume wo Minai ), the television anime adapting the light novel series' "University Student Arc," premiered on July 5. Crunchyroll streamed the anime as it aired, and is also streaming an English dub .

The original novel series has over 3 million copies in circulation. The Yen On imprint of Yen Press publishes the novel series, and Yen Press publishes Tsugumi Nanamiya 's manga adaptation. Yen On will publish the Rascal Does Not Dream of a Dear Friend novel volume on January 6.

title: 'Gensou Suikoden' Announces Supporting Cast
content:
The Gensou Suikoden ( Suikoden: The Anime ) White Stage at AnimeJapan 2026 revealed supporting cast members and a fourth key visual (pictured) on Sunday. The anime series adapting Konami's fantasy role-playing game will begin airing in October 2026.

Cast Culgan: Jun Kasama ( Revenger ) Seed: Yoshitsugu Matsuoka ( Re:Zero kara Hajimeru Isekai Seikatsu ) Leknaat: Megumi Han ( Tate no Yuusha no Nariagari ) Gengen: Shouta Hayama ( Tokyo Revengers ) Rikimaru: Ryuunosuke Watanuki ( Blue Lock ) Rina: Saya Hitomi ( Chi. Chikyuu no Undou ni Tsuite ) Eilie: Umino Mizutama Bolgan: Yuusuke Sasaki ( Dandadan )

Yuuzou Satou ( Exception ) is helming the anime at Konami animation . Michihiro Tsuchiya ( Major 2nd 2nd Season ) is handling the series composition and Ryou Yamauchi ( Uchi no Shishou wa Shippo ga Nai ) is designing the characters. Kouji Nakamura ( Eureka Seven AO ) is composing the music.

The Suikoden franchise debuted in 1995 on the PlayStation, drawing inspiration from the classic Chinese novel Shui Hu Zhuan ( Water Margin ). Konami released Gensou Suikoden II ( Suikoden II ) in December 1998, followed by three more games in the main series in July 2002, August 2004, and February 2006 as well as multiple spin-offs.

Shinjirou Hori penned a light novel adaptation under Kadokawa's Dengeki Bunko imprint with illustrations by Fumi Ishikawa from December 2000 to September 2001.
author: MyAnimeList

The Suikoden franchise debuted in 1995 on the PlayStation, drawing inspiration from the classic Chinese novel Shui Hu Zhuan ( Water Margin ). Konami released Gensou Suikoden II ( Suikoden II ) in December 1998, followed by three more games in the main series in July 2002, August 2004, and February 2006 as well as multiple spin-offs.
Shinjirou Hori penned a light novel adaptation under Kadokawa's Dengeki Bunko imprint with illustrations by Fumi Ishikawa from December 2000 to September 2001.

title: 'Rakudai Kenja no Gakuin Musou' Announces Additional Cast, Teaser Promo
content:
The Rakudai Kenja no Gakuin Musou: Nidome no Tensei, S-Rank Cheat Majutsushi Boukenroku ( The Failed Sage's Academy Domination ) AnimeJapan 2026 stage event at the NBCUniversal booth revealed additional cast and a teaser promotional video on Sunday. The anime series adapting Arata Shiraishi and Kentarou 's fantasy adventure manga will begin airing on Tokyo MX, ABC TV, BS Fuji, WOWOW in July 2026.

Cast Ephtal (Sage/Old Man): Hiroki Touchi ( Sousou no Frieren ) Ephtal (Childhood): Ayaka Fukuhara ( Kawaii dake ja Nai Shikimori-san ) Ilya: Emiri Katou ( Overlord ) Fraser: Daisuke Hirakawa ( Ore dake Level Up na Ken )

Hisashi Ishii ( Yuusha, Yamemasu ) is directing the anime at EMT Squared . Deko Akao ( Saigo ni Hitotsu dake Onegai shitemo Yoroshii deshou ka ) is handling the series composition, and Hideki Furukawa ( Strike the Blood III ) is designing the characters.

Shiraishi began writing the adventure fantasy story on the Shousetsuka ni Narou website in 2018. Kadokawa published the light novel with illustrations by Denim Uo under the Sneaker Bunko imprint in eight volumes from October 2019 to March 2022.

The manga adaptation by Kentarou began its serialization on the Manga UP! website in September 2019. Square Enix released the eighth volume in June 2025.

Square Enix publishes the manga in English on the Manga UP! and Comikey websites.

Teaser PV
